name: Tunisia
avoid: July-August gets punishingly hot in Tozeur and the Sahara fringe even if Tunis still feels manageable.
Louages are efficient but leave only when full, so fixed-timetable planning can unravel on busy weekends.
Many archaeological sites have limited shade, so start early and carry cash, water, and sun protection.
stopoverNote: Tunis-Carthage Airport is the obvious gateway from India; keep the north-coast trio of Tunis, Sidi Bou Said, and Carthage together before using a Tunisair domestic hop or a long overland leg south.

cities.name: Tunis
cities.notes: Bardo Museum, the old medina, French-era boulevards and easy rail day trips into Tunisia's Roman heartland.

cities.name: Sidi Bou Said
cities.notes: Blue-and-white clifftop lanes, café terraces, art spaces and postcard Mediterranean views above the bay.

cities.name: Carthage
cities.notes: Byrsa Hill, Antonine Baths, Punic and Roman layers, and one of North Africa's most atmospheric archaeological spreads.

cities.name: Djerba
cities.notes: Houmt Souk markets, Djerbahood street art, beaches, El Ghriba synagogue and easy-going island stays.

cities.name: Tozeur
cities.notes: Palm oasis walks, brick medina lanes, Chott el Jerid mirages and desert excursions to Ong Jemel and Mos Espa.

itinerary.sim: Tunisie Telecom, Ooredoo Tunisia, and Orange Tunisia all sell airport or city-centre SIMs; expect roughly ₹650–₹1,560 for about 10–25GB, while an eSIM is the easiest backup for landing connected.

connections.method: TGM suburban train or quick taxi ride
TGM hop or taxi along the coast
Tunisair domestic flight from Tunis-Carthage to Tozeur-Nefta
Louage/shared taxi via Gabes or a direct private transfer

extras: The official museum and archaeological-site tariff was updated in April 2026, so Bardo, Carthage, El Jem, and Dougga are pricier than many older blog posts suggest.
Use Tunisair if you want to connect the north coast with Tozeur or Djerba efficiently; overland southbound days are long even when roads are good.
SNCFT is most useful on the Tunis-Sousse-Sfax-El Jem corridor, while louages are the practical fallback once you head inland or need flexible departures.
Carry cash for louages, small museum kiosks, medina cafés, and the informal tip or parking ask at Mos Espa near Ong Jemel.
March to June and September to November are the sweet spots; sand-laden wind and intense midday heat make Sahara-facing days far less pleasant in high summer.
